summary_sections.title: Key Concepts: The Greenhouse Effect
summary_sections.content_html:
The greenhouse effect is a natural process that warms Earth's surface:

  • Sunlight (short-wave radiation) passes through the atmosphere and warms Earth's surface.
  • Earth re-emits energy as infrared (long-wave) radiation.
  • Greenhouse gases (CO₂, CH₄, N₂O, H₂O vapor, O₃) absorb and re-emit infrared radiation, trapping heat in the atmosphere.
  • Without the greenhouse effect, Earth's average temperature would be about -18°C instead of +15°C.
  • The enhanced greenhouse effect: human activities (burning fossil fuels, deforestation, agriculture) increase greenhouse gas concentrations, trapping more heat and causing global warming.
